<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(observer);

    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(target);

    reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserver(target, observer);

    target->changed();

    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    if (enableSafety) {
        thread_manager.enableThreadSafety(true);
    }
    
    return thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 2: Add nodes to the graph
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserverNode(node1);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserverNode(node2);

    // Step 3: Create a batch ID
    auto batchId = ObserverGraph::getInstance().createBatch();

    // Step 4: Register nodes to the batch
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNodeToActiveBatch(node1, batchId);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNodeToActiveBatch(node2, batchId);

    // Step 5: Verify nodes are in the batch
    if (!ObserverGraph::getInstance().isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) ||
        !ObserverGraph::getInstance().isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 6: Unregister the batch
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().closeActiveBatch(batchId);

    // Step 7: Verify nodes are no longer in the batch
    if (ObserverGraph::getInstance().isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) ||

    // Step 8: Return success
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test node closure functionality
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the observer node
 * @return bool Whether node closure worked correctly (relationships cleaned up)
 */
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto source = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    // Add observer relationship: observer observes source
    graph.addObserver(observer, source);

    // Notify source, which should trigger observer's valueChanged
    source->notify();
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset observer's state
    observer->reset();

    // Close the observer node
    observer->close();

    // Notify source again
    source->notify();

    // Check that observer wasn't notified again
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of the test_transactional_update function
/**
 * @brief Tests transactional observer updates to ensure cycles are prevented.
 *
 * @param nodeName The name of the source node to create and test with.
 * @return true if the transactional update correctly prevented a cycle and preserved the original state.
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship (source observes target)
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Step 4: Notify source and verify target reacts
    source->valueChanged();
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Reset nodes for next test
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Step 6: Attempt transactional update that creates a cycle
    try {
        // Transaction: target observes source (creates cycle)
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(target, {source}, {});
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        // Expected exception due to cycle detection
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        // Unexpected exception type
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Notify source again to verify original relationship remains
    source->valueChanged();

    // Step 9: Verify target's state after failed transaction
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 2) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 10: Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// Existing TestMultipleObserver class definition...

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.emplace_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // 6. Verify notifications
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    // Retrieve observers from graph
    auto graphObservers = graph.getObservers(target);
    
    // Check observer count consistency
    if (graphObservers.size() != observers.size()) {
        allNotified = false;
    } else {
        for (auto& wp : graphObservers) {
            if (auto ptr = wp.lock()) [[likely]] {
                totalCalls += ptr->getCallCount();
                if (ptr->getCallCount() != 1 || !ptr->wasChanged()) {
                    allNotified = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return results
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // Create observer nodes and add them to the graph
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(target);

    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(observer1);

    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addNode(observer2);

    // Set up observer dependencies
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserver(target, observer1);
    ObserverGraph::getInstance().addObserver(observer1, observer2);

    // Trigger value change on the target
    target->valueChanged();

    // Verify observer notifications
    bool chainOk = true;
    int totalCalls = 0;

    // Check observer1
    if (auto locked_observer1 = ObserverGraph::getInstance().getNodeByName(observer1Name)) {
        chainOk &= locked_observer1->wasChanged();
        totalCalls += locked_observer1->getCallCount();
    } else {
        chainOk = false;
    }

    // Check observer2
    if (auto locked_observer2 = ObserverGraph::getInstance().getNodeByName(observer2Name)) {
        chainOk &= locked_observer2->wasChanged();
        totalCalls += locked_observer2->getCallCount();
    } else {
        chainOk = false;
    }

    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, chainOk);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_expression(int a, int b) {
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr::create(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr::create(b);
    auto sum = reaction::BinaryOpExpr::create(varA, reaction::AddOp{}, varB);
    auto expr = reaction::BinaryOpExpr::create(sum, reaction::MulOp{}, 2);
    return expr->getValue();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <atomic>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_action(int initialValue, int newValue) {
    Var<int> var(initialValue);
    std::atomic<int> captured(0);

    Action action([&captured](const Var<int>& v) {
        captured.store(v.get());
    }, var);

    var.set(newValue);
    return captured.load();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr<int>(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr<int>(b);

    // Create first calculation (sum of varA and varB)
    auto calc1 = reaction::CalcExpr<int>([=]() {
        return varA->get() + varB->get();
    });

    // Create second calculation (double of calc1)
    auto calc2 = reaction::CalcExpr<int>([=]() {
        return calc1->get() * 2;
    });

    // Update varA to newA
    varA->set(newA);

    // Return the final value of calc2
    return calc2->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

PersonRecord::PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
    : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

const std::string& PersonRecord::getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
int PersonRecord::getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

void PersonRecord::setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
void PersonRecord::setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

/**
 * @brief Tests reactive updates by modifying a PersonRecord's fields and observing a dependent calculation.
 *
 * @param initialName Starting name of the person.
 * @param initialAge Starting age of the person.
 * @param newName New name to update with.
 * @param newAge New age to update with.
 * @return A string combining the updated name and age.
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord pr(initialName, initialAge);
    
    auto calc = reaction::make_calc([this](const std::string& name, int age) {
        return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
    }, pr.m_name, pr.m_age);
    
    pr.setName(newName);
    pr.setAge(newAge);
    
    return calc->get();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

#include <set>
#include <shared_mutex>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <vector>

namespace reaction {

// Forward declarations
class Node;
using NodePtr = std::shared_ptr<Node>;

/**
 * @brief Reactive graph node base class.
 *
 * All reactive nodes should inherit from this base.
 * It manages notification propagation and observer lists.
 */
class Node : public std::enable_shared_from_this<Node> {
public:
    virtual ~Node() = default;

    /**
     * @brief Trigger downstream notifications.
     *
     * By default, triggers notify().
     * @param changed Whether the node's value has changed.
     */
    virtual void valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
        this->notify(changed);
    }

    /**
     * @brief Handle value change without triggering downstream notifications.
     *
     * This method is called when a node's value changes but we don't want
     * to immediately propagate the change to observers. This is useful in
     * batch operations where notifications are deferred until the end.
     *
     * @param changed Whether the node's value has actually changed (default: true)
     */
    virtual void changedNoNotify([[maybe_unused]] bool changed = true) {}

    /**
     * @brief Update the depth of this node in the reactive dependency graph.
     *
     * The depth represents how many levels deep this node is in the dependency chain.
     * This is used for ordering nodes during batch operations and cycle detection.
     * The depth is set to the maximum of the current depth and the provided depth.
     *
     * @param depth The new depth value to consider
     */
    void updateDepth(uint16_t depth) noexcept {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        if (ThreadManager::getInstance().isThreadSafetyEnabled()) {
            // Use atomic compare-and-swap for thread-safe depth updates
            uint16_t currentDepth = m_depth.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
            uint16_t newDepth = std::max(depth, currentDepth);
            while (newDepth > currentDepth && 
                   !m_depth.compare_exchange_weak(currentDepth, newDepth, std::memory_order_relaxed)) {
                newDepth = std::max(depth, currentDepth);
            }
        } else {
            // Single-threaded: direct assignment
            m_depth.store(std::max(depth, m_depth.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)), std::memory_order_relaxed);
        }
    }

    /**
     * @brief Update all observer dependencies at once.
     *
     * Resets current observers, then adds given nodes as new observers.
     * @tparam Args Parameter pack for node pointers.
     * @param args Nodes to observe.
     */
    template <typename... Args>
    void updateObservers(Args &&...args);

    /**
     * @brief Add a single observer node.
     * @param node Observer node to add.
     */
    void addOneObserver(const NodePtr &node);

    /**
     * @brief Notify observers and delayed repeat nodes.
     * @param changed Whether the node's value has changed.
     */
    void notify(bool changed = true) {
        if (ThreadManager::getInstance().isThreadSafetyEnabled()) {
            // Copy observers under lock to avoid holding lock during callbacks
            std::multiset<std::shared_ptr<Node>> observersCopy;
            {
                ConditionalSharedLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(m_observersMutex);
                observersCopy.insert(m_observers.begin(), m_observers.end());
            }

            // Notify observers outside of lock to prevent deadlocks
            for (auto &observer : observersCopy) {
                if (auto wp = observer.lock()) [[likely]]
                    wp->valueChanged(changed);
            }
        } else {
            // Single-threaded mode: no need to copy, direct iteration
            for (auto &observer : m_observers) {
                if (auto wp = observer.lock()) [[likely]]
                    wp->valueChanged(changed);
            }
        }
    }

private:
    std::atomic<uint16_t> m_depth{0};               ///< Depth of the node in reactive chain.
    mutable ConditionalSharedMutex m_observersMutex; ///< Conditional mutex for thread-safe observers access.
    std::multiset<std::shared_ptr<Node>> m_observers; ///< Direct observers of this node.

    template <typename... Args>
    friend class VarExprBase;

    friend struct BatchCompare;
    friend class Batch;
    friend struct FilterTrig;
    friend struct std::hash<reaction::Node>;
};

} // namespace reaction

namespace std {

using namespace reaction;
template <>
struct hash<reaction::Node> {
    std::size_t operator()(const Node *node) const noexcept {
        return std::hash<reaction::Node *>()(node);
    }
};

} // namespace std

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(valueA, "varA");
    auto varB = std::make_shared<reaction::Var<int>>(valueB, "varB");
    
    // Create a calculation with name "calcAB"
    auto calcAB = std::make_shared<reaction::Calc<int>>();
    calcAB->setSource([=]() { return varA->get() + varB->get(); }, "calcAB");
    
    // Create an action with name "actionChange"
    auto actionChange = std::make_shared<reaction::Action>();
    actionChange->setName("actionChange");
    
    // Collect the names into a combined string
    std::ostringstream oss;
    oss << varA->getName() << ", " << varB->getName() << ", " << calcAB->getName() << ", " << actionChange->getName();
    
    return oss.str();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive graph
    auto graph = std::make_shared<Graph>();

    // 1. Create reactive variable (sensor)
    auto sensorNode = graph->createNode();
    VarExpr<double> sensorVar(sensorNode, initialValue);

    // 2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
    VarExpr<int> recomputeCount(graph->createNode(), 0);
    // Last processed value isn't needed here, but maybe we can ignore it.

    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation (status)
    VarExpr<std::string> statusVar(graph->createNode());
    statusVar.setExpr([levelThreshold = levelThreshold](const NodePtr &node) {
        auto val = node->getValue<double>();
        return val > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });

    // 4. Apply filter (ChangeTrigFilter with diffThreshold)
    ChangeTrigFilter<double> changeFilter(diffThreshold);
    statusVar.setTrigMode(FilterTrig(changeFilter));

    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensorVar.updateValue(firstUpdate);

    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount.getValue();

    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensorVar.updateValue(secondUpdate);

    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount.getValue();

    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusVar.getValue();

    // 10. Return the tuple
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    auto graph = std::make_shared<Graph>();
    
    // 1. Create reactive sensor variable
    auto sensorNode = graph->createNode();
    VarExpr<double> sensorVar(sensorNode, initialValue);
    
    // 2. Track recomputation count
    VarExpr<int> recomputeCount(graph->createNode(), 0);
    
    // 3. Create status calculation with filter trigger
    VarExpr<std::string> statusVar(graph->createNode());
    statusVar.setExpr([levelThreshold](const NodePtr& node) {
        return sensorVar.getPtr()->getValue() > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    
    // 4. Apply change-trigger filter
    ChangeTrigFilter<double> changeFilter(diffThreshold);
    statusVar.setTrigMode(FilterTrig(changeFilter));
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensorVar.updateValue(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record first recompute count
    int count1 = recomputeCount.getValue();
    
    // 7. Update with second value
    sensorVar.updateValue(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record second count
    int count2 = recomputeCount.getValue();
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string status = statusVar.getValue();
    
    return std::make_tuple(status, count1, count2);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    m_notificationCount++;
}

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount
inline int FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount() const {
    return m_notificationCount;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields constructor
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus = true)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

// Implementation of ProfileFields::score and active accessors
inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() noexcept { return m_score; }
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() noexcept { return m_active; }

// Implementation of test_field_binding
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    auto profile = std::make_shared<ProfileFields>(initialScore);
    auto node = std::make_shared<FieldOwnerNode>();
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(node);
    auto fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindFields(profile->score(), node);
    fieldGraph.bindFields(profile->active(), node);
    profile->score() += scoreDelta;
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = node->getNotificationCount();
    profile->active() = true;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = node->getNotificationCount();
    return FieldBindingResult{
        notificationsAfterScoreUpdate,
        notificationsAfterStatusUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of RecordingNode methods
class RecordingNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notificationCount() const noexcept {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    std::atomic<int> m_notifications{0}; ///< Counter for notifications received
};

// Implementation of the test function
RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 1: Create nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    observerGraph.addNode(node1);
    observerGraph.addNode(node2);
    
    // Step 2: Set initial dependency (node2 observes node1)
    node2->addObCb(node1, nullptr);
    
    // Step 3: Save current state for rollback
    reaction::Transaction originalTx([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node, const reaction::ObserverList& observers) {
        // Transaction captures current observer state automatically
    });
    
    // Step 4: Replace dependencies transactionally
    {
        reaction::Transaction newTx([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node, const reaction::ObserverList& observers) {
            // Reset node2's observers
            auto locked_node2 = node2.lock();
            if (!locked_node2) [[unlikely]] return;
            locked_node2->removeObservers();
            
            // Create and add new node3
            auto node3 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
            observerGraph.addNode(node3);
            
            // ObCb with nullptr filter (always triggers)
            locked_node2->addObCb(node3, nullptr);
        });
        newTx.commit();
    }
    
    // Step 5: Trigger notification on node3 (second sensor)
    auto node3 = node2->getObservers().front(); // Get the new observer (node3)
    node3->notify();
    
    // Step 6: Record count after second binding
    int afterSecond = node2->notificationCount();
    
    // Step 7: Rollback to original state
    originalTx.rollback();
    
    // Step 8: Trigger notification on original node1
    node1->notify();
    
    // Step 9: Record count after rollback
    int afterRollback = node2->notificationCount();
    
    return RollbackObservation{
        .notificationsAfterSecondBinding = afterSecond,
        .notificationsAfterRollback = afterRollback
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

NamedObserverNode::NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
const std::string& NamedObserverNode::name() const { return m_name; }

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    auto source = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("source");
    auto direct = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("direct");
    auto transitive = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("transitive");

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(source);
    graph.registerNode(direct);
    graph.registerNode(transitive);

    graph.addObserver(source, direct);
    graph.addObserver(direct, transitive);

    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    graph.collectObservers(source, observersSet);

    bool hasDirect = false;
    bool hasTransitive = false;
    for (const auto& wp : observersSet) {
        if (auto ptr = wp.lock()) {
            if (ptr->name() == "direct") hasDirect = true;
            if (ptr->name() == "transitive") hasTransitive = true;
        }
    }

    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.registerThread();

    size_t beforeCount = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    std::thread worker([]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread();
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::seconds(1));
    });

    worker.join();

    size_t afterCount = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfter = safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBefore = beforeCount,
        .threadCountAfter = afterCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive variable
    VarExpr<int> value(baseValue);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    VarExpr<int> recomputeCount(0);
    
    // Create calculation that triggers on value change and increments the count
    CalcExpr<int> calc([this](const int& val) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return val; // Return unchanged value to avoid altering it
    }, value);
    
    // Capture current recompute count before batch execution
    int before = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Execute batch of updates to 'value'
    {
        BatchOp batch([this]() {
            value += deltaA;
            value += deltaB;
        }, {value});
    }
    
    // Capture recompute count after batch execution
    int after = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Retrieve final value of 'value'
    int final = value.get();
    
    // Return results
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = before,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = after,
        .finalValue = final
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

void CacheProbeNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    // Implementation as per requirements; triggers cache updates
    ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
}

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(nodeA);
    graph.registerNode(nodeB);
    graph.registerNode(nodeC);
    graph.registerNode(nodeD);

    nodeA->addObCb(nodeB); // Direct observer
    nodeB->addObCb(nodeC); // Direct observer
    nodeC->addObCb(nodeD); // Direct observer (transitive for nodeA)

    // Populate cache by triggering value change
    nodeB->valueChanged();
    nodeC->valueChanged();

    // Collect observers (ensures cache usage)
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    graph.getObservers(nodeA, observers);

    auto statsBefore = graph.getStats();
    graph.triggerCleanup();
    auto statsAfter = graph.getStats();

    graph.closeNode(nodeA);
    graph.closeNode(nodeB);
    graph.closeNode(nodeC);
    graph.closeNode(nodeD);

    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = statsBefore.m_graphEntries.load(std::memory_order_relaxed),
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = statsAfter.m_graphEntries.load(std::memory_order_relaxed),
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = statsAfter.m_graphHitRatio.load(std::memory_order_relaxed)};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    reaction::ThreadManager& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();

    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // 4. Get thread count
    auto thread_count = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    auto before_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    auto after_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again

    auto after_reset = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 10. Return transition

        .afterReset = after_reset,
        .threadCount = thread_count

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::VarExpr::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::ConstExpr::create(constValue);
    
    // Create a reactive calculation that sums both variables
    auto sumOp = reaction::AddOp{mutableVar, constVar};
    auto sumCalc = reaction::CalcExpr::create(sumOp);
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sumCalc->getValue();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar->setValue(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sumCalc->getValue();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([&]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g;
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBeforeGuard,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfterGuard,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBeforeGuard,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of IdTrackedFields with automatic unique ID generation
class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
private:
    static uint64_t global_counter; // Class-wide counter for unique IDs
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : m_value(value) {
        // Generate unique ID using atomic increment
        this->setId(global_counter++);
    }

    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() noexcept { return m_value; }
    const reaction::Var<int>& valueField() const noexcept { return m_value; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_value; ///< Reactive variable storing the input value
};

// Initialize static member variable
uint64_t IdTrackedFields::global_counter{0};

// Structure to hold comparison results
struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;

    FieldIdComparison(uint64_t f1, uint64_t f2, uint64_t s)
        : firstIdFirstRead(f1), firstIdSecondRead(f2), secondId(s) {}
};

// Test function implementation
FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);

    // Read IDs and verify stability
    uint64_t firstId1 = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstId2 = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();

    // Update reactive fields to suppress compiler warnings
    reaction::Var<int> val1 = first.valueField();
    reaction::Var<int> val2 = second.valueField();

    return FieldIdComparison{firstId1, firstId2, secondId};
};

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    auto var = VarExpr<int>::create(initialValue);
    auto calc = CalcExpr<LastHandle<int>>::create(
        [this](const NodePtr& node) {
            return node->as<VarExpr<int>>()->get();
        },
        LastHandle{}
    );

    int initial = calc->get();
    var->set(updatedValue);
    int updated = calc->get();
    var->close();
    int closed = calc->get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initial,
        .afterUpdate = updated,
        .afterSourceClose = closed
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    bool changed = reaction::atomicAdd(resource, delta);
    return ResourceAtomicResult{
        .finalValue = resource.getValue(),
        .operationReportedChange = changed
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report;

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();

    // 3. Create report struct (done)

    // 4. Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = StackMonitor::getInstance().isMonitoringEnabled();

    // 5. Create StackGuard in a scope block
    {
        StackGuard<sizeof(std::thread_local char[1024])> guard;
        // 6. Check if guard was allocated
        report.guardAllocated = guard.getPtr() != nullptr;

        // 7. Get current usage during guard
        report.usageDuringGuard = StackMonitor::getInstance().getCurrentUsage();

        // 8. Get statistics
        const StackMonitorStats& stats = StackMonitor::getInstance().getStats();
        report.usagePercentage = stats.usagePercentage;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.nearLimit;
    }

    // 11. Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = StackMonitor::getInstance().getCurrentUsage();

    // 12. Disable monitoring again
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 13. Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = StackMonitor::getInstance().isMonitoringEnabled();

    // 14. Return report
    return report;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Create lambda to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed = largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create small and large SBOResources
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource{initialSmall};
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource{createLargePayload()};

    // Update small resource and check if change is reported
    bool updateReportedChange = smallResource.update(updatedSmall);

    // Retrieve storage information
    StorageInfo smallStorage = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    StorageInfo largeStorage = largeResource.getStorageInfo();

    // Compile status
    SboStorageStatus status{
        .updateReportedChange = updateReportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallStorage.isUsingSBO(),
        .smallStackUsage = smallStorage.stackUsage(),
        .largeInitialized = largeStorage.isInitialized(),
        .largeUsingSBO = largeStorage.isUsingSBO()
    };

    return status;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety{false};
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety{false};
    bool trySharedWithSafety{false};
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety{false};
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();

    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;

    // Test without safety
    {
        bool shared_acquired = mutex.trySharedNoSafety();
        SharedMutexResult result{
            .trySharedWithoutSafety = shared_acquired
        };
        if (shared_acquired) {
            mutex.unlockShared();
        }

        {
            reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
            bool unique_acquired = mutex.tryUniqueNoSafety();
            result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = unique_acquired;
            if (unique_acquired) {
                mutex.unlockUnique();
            }
        }
    }

    // Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();

    // Test with safety
    {
        bool shared_acquired = mutex.tryShared();
        SharedMutexResult result{
            .trySharedWithSafety = shared_acquired
        };
        if (shared_acquired) {
            mutex.unlockShared();
        }

        {
            reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
            bool unique_acquired = mutex.tryUnique();
            result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = unique_acquired;
            if (unique_acquired) {
                mutex.unlockUnique();
            }
        }
    }

    return SharedMutexResult{};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo.h>
#include <memory_config.h>

namespace test_reaction {

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    auto tiny_traits = reaction::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::getInstance();
    auto medium_traits = reaction::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::getInstance();
    auto large_traits = reaction::SBOTraits<LargePod>::getInstance();
    auto config = reaction::SBOTraitsBase::getInstance().getConfig();
    
    return SboTraitSummary{
        .tinyAlwaysSbo = tiny_traits.m_always_sbo,
        .tinyForceHeap = tiny_traits.m_force_heap,
        .mediumConditionalSbo = medium_traits.m_conditional_sbo,
        .mediumForceHeap = medium_traits.m_force_heap,
        .largeForceHeap = large_traits.m_force_heap,
        .alwaysThreshold = config.m_sbo_always_threshold,
        .conditionalThreshold = config.m_sbo_conditional_threshold,
        .maxStackUsage = config.m_sbo_max_stack_usage
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(

    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    for (const auto &code : codes) {

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    reaction::ReactionException ex(message, code, file, line, function);
    ExceptionInfo info{
        .formattedMessage = ex.getFormattedMessage(),
        .originalMessage = ex.getOriginalMessage(),
        .file = ex.getFile(),
        .line = ex.getLine(),
        .function = ex.getFunction(),
        .codeName = ex.getCodeName()
    };
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourcePointerSnapshot {
    int initialPointerValue;
    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;
};

template <typename T>
using Resource = reaction::core::Resource<T>;

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    Resource<int> res(initialValue);
    auto ptr1 = res.getPtr();
    int initial = *ptr1;
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    auto ptr2 = res.getPtr();
    int after = *ptr2;
    bool stable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initial,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = after,
        .pointerStable = stable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct Counter {
    int value;
    void add(int delta) noexcept { value += delta; }
};

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    auto counter = std::make_shared<Counter>(initialValue);
    reaction::VarExpr<Counter> var(counter);

    auto ptr1 = var.getPtr().get();
    ptr1->add(delta);
    int val1 = var.get();

    auto ptr2 = var.getPtr().get();
    ptr2->add(delta);
    int val2 = var.get();

    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;

    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = val1,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = val2,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    ValiditySnapshot snapshot;

    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    auto original = std::make_shared<ReactVar<int>>(initialValue);

    // Step 2: Create copy of source
    auto copy = std::make_shared<ReactVar<int>>(original->getValue());

    // Step 3: Check validity before close
    snapshot.firstValidBeforeClose = !original->shared_from_this()->isClosed();

    // Step 4: Check equality before close
    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = (*original == *copy);
    } catch (...) {
        snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = false;
    }

    // Step 5: Close source node
    original->close();

    // Step 6: Check validity after close
    snapshot.firstValidAfterClose = !original->shared_from_this()->isClosed();

    // Step 7: Check equality after close with exception handling
    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (*original == *copy);
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    } catch (...) {
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    }

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    std::set<ReactPtr> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(std::make_shared<Var>(firstName));
    reactSet.insert(std::make_shared<Var>(secondName));
    reactSet.insert(std::make_shared<Var>(thirdName));

    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& ptr : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(ptr->getName());
    }

    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = (uniqueNames.size() == orderedNames.size());

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of ResetAwareNode
ResetAwareNode::ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(name), m_notificationCount(0) {
}

int ResetAwareNode::notifications() const {
    return m_notificationCount;
}

void ResetAwareNode::clear() {
    m_notificationCount = 0;
}

// Assuming ObserverNode has a reset() method (common in reactive node implementations)
void ResetAwareNode::reset() {
    // Reset any internal state, but keep observer dependencies
    m_notificationCount = 0; // Reset the notification counter
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

namespace test_reaction {

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName, const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addNode(source);
    graph->addNode(target);

    graph->addObserver(source, target);

    source->notify();

    int beforeReset = target->notifications();
    bool notifiedBefore = beforeReset > 0;

    target->clear();

    target->reset(); // Reset the target node

    source->notify();

    int afterReset = target->notifications();
    bool notifiedAfter = afterReset > 0;

    graph->closeNode(source);
    graph->closeNode(target);

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>  // Assuming StackMonitor and StackGuard are here
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot result;

    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // Step 3: Check small allocation without monitoring
    result.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(1024);

    // Step 3: Check large allocation without monitoring
    result.largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // Step 4: Enable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();

    // Step 5: Reinitialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // Step 6: Check large allocation with monitoring
    result.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // Step 7: Create StackGuard in scope
    {
        StackGuard g(largeSize);
        // Step 8: Check if guard succeeded
        result.guardSucceeded = g.isSuccess();  // Assuming StackGuard has an isSuccess() method
    }

    // Step 9: Check if allocation is blocked at limit
    result.allocationBlockedAtLimit = !StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // Step 11: Check runtime allocation after release
    result.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Notes:**
1. Assumes `StackMonitor` has methods `getInstance()`, `disable()`, `enable()`, `init()`, and `canAllocate(size_t)`.
2. Assumes `StackGuard` has a constructor that attempts to allocate the given size and a method `isSuccess()` to check if the allocation succeeded.
3. The `largeSize` parameter is used for both the initial large allocation check and the StackGuard creation.
4. The small allocation size is arbitrarily set to 1024 bytes (1KB) for demonstration.
</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)), m_notifications(0) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }
    int notifications() const noexcept { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() noexcept { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    mutable int m_notifications;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    observerGraph.addNode(field);

    fieldGraph.registerObjField(objectId, field);

    observerGraph.addObCb(owner, field);

    // Trigger value change to notify owner

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <typeinfo>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    VoidResourceSnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<reaction::Void>>();
    
    // 2. Check if resource uses SBO storage
    snapshot.usesSboStorage = resource->isUsingSbo();
    
    // 3. Get value from resource
    auto val = resource->getValue();
    
    // 4. Check if retrieved value is Void type
    snapshot.retrievedIsVoid = typeid(val) == typeid(reaction::Void);
    
    // 5. Get observer graph instance
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Register resource node
    observerGraph->registerNode(resource);
    
    // 7. Assign node name
    observerGraph->setName(resource, nodeName);
    
    // 8. Check if name was assigned
    snapshot.nameAssigned = observerGraph->getName(resource) == nodeName;
    
    // 9. Close node
    observerGraph->closeNode(resource);
    
    // 10. Check if name was cleared after close
    snapshot.nameClearedAfterClose = observerGraph->getName(resource).empty();
    
    // 11. Return void resource snapshot
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto co = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto src1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto src2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto src3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();

    reaction::ObserverGraph &g = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    g.registerNode(src1);
    g.registerNode(src2);
    g.registerNode(src3);

    src1->addObserver(co);

    // Trigger first source
    src1->notify(true);

    int firstAfterInitial = co->notifications();

    // Rewire dependencies
    src1->removeObserver(co);
    src2->addObserver(co);
    src3->addObserver(co);

    // Trigger old source (no change)
    src1->notify(true);
    int firstAfterUpdate = co->notifications();

    // Trigger second source
    src2->notify(true);
    int secondAfterUpdate = co->notifications();

    // Trigger third source
    src3->notify(true);
    int thirdAfterUpdate = co->notifications();

    // Close nodes
    src1->close();
    src2->close();
    src3->close();
    co->close();

    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = firstAfterInitial,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstAfterUpdate,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = secondAfterUpdate,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = thirdAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    Var<int> var(5);
    Var<int> counter(0);
    auto calc = createCalc(varExpr(var), [this](const int& newValue) {
        counter() += 1;
    });

    int afterAddition = var.atomicAddAssign(3);
    int afterMultiplication = var.atomicMultiplyAssign(2);
    int afterBitwiseAnd = var.atomicBitwiseAndAssign(0xF);
    int afterShift = var.atomicLeftShiftAssign(2);
    int postIncrementReturn = var.postIncrement();
    int finalValue = var.get();
    int calcEvaluations = counter.get();

    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAddition,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMultiplication,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterBitwiseAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postIncrementReturn,
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .calcEvaluations = calcEvaluations
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate unary expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function showcases how low-level expression builder helpers (`make_binary_op`,
 * `make_unary_op`) and high-level unary operators (`-`, `!`, `~`) cooperate with the
 * `expr` factory to produce reactive expressions.
 *
 * @param a First input value participating in the sum expression
 * @param b Second input value participating in the sum expression
 * @return UnaryExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of expression outputs before/after updates
 */
inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    auto a_var = VarExpr<int>::create(a);
    auto b_var = VarExpr<int>::create(b);

    // Step 2: Build negated sum expression
    auto sum_expr = make_add_op(a_var, b_var);
    auto negated_sum_expr = make_unary_op(NegOp{}, sum_expr);
    auto negated_sum_node = createReactiveNode(negated_sum_expr);

    // Step 3: Create reactive boolean variable
    auto c_var = VarExpr<bool>::create(true);

    // Step 4: Build logical NOT expression
    auto not_c_expr = make_unary_op(NotOp{}, c_var);
    auto not_c_node = createReactiveNode(not_c_expr);

    // Step 5: Create reactive integer variable
    auto d_var = VarExpr<int>::create(5);

    // Step 6: Build bitwise NOT expression
    auto bitwise_not_d_expr = make_unary_op(BitwiseNotOp{}, d_var);
    auto bitwise_not_d_node = createReactiveNode(bitwise_not_d_expr);

    // Step 7: Get initial values
    int initialNegatedSum = negated_sum_node.get();
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip = not_c_node.get();
    int bitwiseNotValue = bitwise_not_d_node.get();

    // Step 8: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    a_var.set(3);
    b_var.set(4);
    c_var.set(false);
    d_var.set(6);

    // Step 9: Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = negated_sum_node.get();
    bool updatedLogicalNot = not_c_node.get();
    int updatedBitwiseNot = bitwise_not_d_node.get();

    // Step 10: Return unary expression snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = updatedLogicalNot,
        .bitwiseNotValue = updatedBitwiseNot
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate logical expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function shows how high-level logical operators (`&&`, `||`) compose reactive
 * expressions that are then lifted into `expr` calculations.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first boolean input
 * @param b Initial value for the second boolean input
 * @param c Initial value for the fallback boolean input
 * @return LogicalExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of AND/OR results before and after updates
 */
inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive boolean variables
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(b);
    auto varC = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(c);

    // Build logical expressions
    auto andExpr = varA && varB;
    auto orExpr = varA || varB;

    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool initialOr = orExpr.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA.set(!a);
    varB.set(!b);

    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool updatedOr = orExpr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    // Step 1: Create NodeMetricsCache instance
    auto cache = std::make_unique<reaction::NodeMetricsCache>();
    
    // Step 2: Create ObserverNode instance
    auto node = std::make_unique<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // Step 3: Get cache statistics before caching
    auto statsBefore = cache->getStats();
    const size_t entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries;
    
    // Step 4: Query metrics before caching (expecting a miss)
    auto metricsBefore = cache->getNodeMetrics(node.get());
    
    // Step 5: Cache node metrics
    cache->cacheNodeMetrics(node.get());
    
    // Step 6: Query metrics after caching (expecting a hit)
    auto metricsAfter = cache->getNodeMetrics(node.get());
    
    // Step 7: Get cache statistics after caching
    auto statsAfterCache = cache->getStats();
    const size_t entriesAfterCache = statsAfterCache.entries;
    const size_t hitCount = statsAfterCache.hitCount;
    const size_t missCount = statsAfterCache.missCount;
    const double hitRatio = static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount);
    
    // Step 8: Invalidate all cache entries
    cache->invalidateAll();
    
    // Step 9: Query metrics after invalidation (expecting a miss)
    auto metricsInvalidated = cache->getNodeMetrics(node.get());
    
    // Step 10: Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto statsAfterInvalidate = cache->getStats();
    const size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalidate.entries;
    const size_t finalMissCount = statsAfterInvalidate.missCount;
    const double finalHitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfterInvalidate.hitCount) / 
                                 (statsAfterInvalidate.hitCount + finalMissCount);
    
    // Step 11: Return the snapshot
    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = entriesBefore,
        .entriesAfterCache = entriesAfterCache,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInvalidate.hitCount,
        .missCount = finalMissCount,
        .hitRatio = finalHitRatio,
        .metricsCachedExists = !metricsAfter.isEmpty(),
        .metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate == 0
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

template <typename Key, typename Value>
class IntCache : public CacheBase<Key, Value> {
public:
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::CacheBase;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::insert;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::lookup;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getStats;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getSize;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    auto cache = std::make_unique<IntCache<int, int>>();
    
    auto initialStats = cache->getStats();
    size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.entries;
    
    auto firstResult = cache->lookup(42);
    bool firstLookupWasMiss = !firstResult.has_value();
    
    cache->insert(42, 100);
    
    auto secondResult = cache->lookup(42);
    bool secondLookupWasHit = secondResult.has_value();
    
    auto statsAfterInsert = cache->getStats();
    size_t entriesAfterInsert = statsAfterInsert.entries;
    
    cache->invalidateAll();
    cache->triggerCleanup();
    
    auto statsAfterInvalidation = cache->getStats();
    size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalidation.entries;
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = entriesBefore,
        .entriesAfterInsert = entriesAfterInsert,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInsert.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterInsert.missCount,
        .hitRatio = statsAfterInsert.hitCount + statsAfterInsert.missCount > 0 
                        ? static_cast<double>(statsAfterInsert.hitCount) / 
                              (statsAfterInsert.hitCount + statsAfterInsert.missCount) 
                        : 0.0,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = firstLookupWasMiss,
        .secondLookupWasHit = secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

GlobalStateSnapshot test_global_state_scoped_guards() {
    // 1. Reset global state
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    // 2. Create initial snapshot
    GlobalStateSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.trackingActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();

    // 3. RegFunGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::RegFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // 4. Check state inside RegFunGuard
        snapshot.trackingActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().isTrackingActive();
        snapshot.batchActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();
    }

    // 5. BatchFunGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::BatchFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // 6. Check state inside BatchFunGuard
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    }

    // 7. BatchExeGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::BatchExeGuard g();
        // 8. Check state inside BatchExeGuard
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    }

    // 9. Reset global state again
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    // 10. Check state after guards
    snapshot.trackingActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

The function should:

2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread

6. Create worker thread and register it

9. Return force register snapshot

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;
    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    auto varA = Var(a);
    auto varB = Var(b);
    auto varC = Var(c);

    // Step 2: Build sum expression (A + B)
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_expr(varA, varB, OpAdd);
    auto sum = expr(sumExpr);

    // Step 3: Build negated expression (- (A + B))
    auto negExpr = make_unary_expr(sumExpr, OpNeg);
    auto negated = expr(negExpr);

    // Step 4: Build chained expression (- (A + B) * C)
    auto chainedExpr = make_binary_expr(negExpr, varC, OpMul);
    auto chained = expr(chainedExpr);

    // Step 5: Get initial values
    RawExpressionSnapshot snapshot{
        .initialSum = sum->get(),
        .initialNegatedSum = negated->get(),
        .initialChainedValue = chained->get()
    };

    // Step 6: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA->set(a + 1);
    varC->set(c + 1);

    // Step 7: Get updated values
    snapshot.updatedSum = sum->get();
    snapshot.updatedNegatedSum = negated->get();
    snapshot.updatedChainedValue = chained->get();

    // Step 8: Return snapshot
    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
